provider_source.source_of_truth_boundary: Provider-native streams are input evidence; the normalized AOF session export is the canonical portable package.

event_summaries.summary: Runtime selected v7.6 Provider-Neutral Session Export as the next release frontier.
AOF framed v7.6 as portable session evidence rather than provider automation or semantic proof.
AOF runtime commands created task, readiness, session, role, team, council, coverage, and export evidence.
Schemas, commands, CLI routing, docs, tests, and release metadata were written for v7.6.
Session export tests and release audits are required before tag.
No active blocker remains; Node 25 local warning is treated as a boundary, not release proof.
Stop if export lacks required event types, source-of-truth boundary, redaction boundary, release-ready boundary, or not-proven limits.

risk_candidates: Provider stream lock-in could hide AI work evidence.
Export completeness could be mistaken for semantic correctness or privacy completeness.
decision_candidates: Promote session-export-audit into release-state-audit for v7.6.0 and later.

redaction_boundary: Export stores summaries and refs only; raw secrets, credentials, and private full prompt bodies are out of scope.
release_ready_boundary: Release-ready export evidence is structural/runtime evidence only.
not_proven: Session export does not prove semantic correctness, universal privacy safety, provider API integration, or external adoption.
